title: Add post-deployment Keystone API health check
summary: Review #2 after addressing external feedback from berendt. Three changes: (1) extracted 7 inline condition type/reason strings into typed constants (W-002), (2) added Chainsaw E2E test with CR fixture (W-001), (3) removed duplicate timeout constant test (I-001). All changes are clean, follow existing patterns, and tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: All unit tests pass (24 health-check-related tests)
Edge cases covered (empty endpoint, timeout, DNS, connection refused, generic error)
Response body close verified for both success and error paths
ObservedGeneration tracked in all condition paths
Chainsaw E2E test validates end-to-end wiring

code_quality_checklist.item: Condition type/reason constants extracted to typed block (W-002)
Constants follow reconcile_networkpolicy.go and reconcile_database.go pattern
No dead code or unused imports
go vet passes with no warnings

security_checklist.item: HTTP client timeout bounded by HealthCheckTimeout
Response body closed via defer in all paths

architecture_checklist.item: Constants co-located with production code in reconcile_healthcheck.go
E2E test follows Chainsaw pattern with SPDX header, JMESPath assertions, catch blocks
CR fixture uses unique name (keystone-healthcheck) for parallel test execution

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated condition strings in production code

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty endpoint detected early before HTTP request
Network errors classified and requeued, not returned as hard errors

defensive_checklist.item: All HTTP error classes handled (timeout, DNS, connection refused, generic)

suggested_improvements: Test assertions in reconcile_healthcheck_test.go use inline string literals (e.g., Equal("APIHealthy")) instead of the newly defined constants (e.g., Equal(conditionReasonAPIHealthy)). This matches the existing pattern in reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go but diverges from the stronger pattern in reconcile_database_test.go. Consider aligning test assertions to use the constants for compile-time safety — but this is not a required change since both patterns exist in the codebase.
